segments.text: Landlord: Hello?
Student: Oh, hello.
Student: I wanted to enquire about the house you're going to let out.
Student: Is it still available?
Landlord: Yes.
Landlord: May I ask where you heard about it?
Landlord: Was it in an advert?
Student: Right, it was.
Landlord: OK, what do you want to know?
Student: Well, I'm going to share the house with two friends.
Student: We've been staying on campus, but we're going to graduate, so we need to find a house as soon as possible.
Student: Anyway, could you tell me the exact date when we can move in?
Landlord: Well, the contract with my current tenant is due next week, on May 3rd.
Landlord: And I need a day to clean and check the things in the house, so I think the house will be ready on 5th May.
Landlord: Is that OK?
Student: Yes, that will be fine.
Student: And what we're most concerned about is the rent.
Student: We'd like something around 500 dollars a month for each of us, but we could go up to 600 dollars if we have to.
Student: But we can't go beyond that.
Landlord: You are lucky – the rent will be 1,700 dollars in total, which is within your budget.
Landlord: You'll need to pay monthly to me, by cash or credit card, I don't mind.
Landlord: And you'll have to pay me 1,500 dollars as a deposit before you move in.
Landlord: The deposit is as insurance, in case you break something.
Student: OK, that's reasonable.
Landlord: Oh, I nearly forgot.
Landlord: One important thing is that you'll be required to provide a credit check to guarantee your ability to pay the rent every month.
Landlord: This will charge you 15 dollars.
Student: No problem.
Student: How about the facilities?
Landlord: First there are three bedrooms combined with two bathrooms.
Landlord: Then there is a shared kitchen which was refurbished several months ago.
Landlord: By the way, do you cook yourselves?
Student: Yeah, we like cooking.
Student: Do we have to buy our own cookers?
Landlord: Basic facilities for cooking are available, but if you want a dishwasher, I'm sorry it needs to be added later.
Student: I think the basic facilities are enough for us.
Student: Is there any place we can park?
Student: As one of my friends has a private car.
Landlord: Yes, there is a garage which not only provides space for your car but also has a special section where you can do some of your work.
Landlord: It's very quiet for you to concentrate.
Student: It sounds so special!
Landlord: There are some other utilities I have to tell you.
Landlord: There is a garden in front of the house, in which you will find some interesting plants and beautiful flowers.
Student: Do we have to take care of these plants and flowers?
Landlord: Well, you don't have to worry about that.
Landlord: There will be a professional gardener to do the landscaping in the garden regularly.
Landlord: But you do need to help water the grass once a week.
Landlord: Is it a problem for you?
Student: Of course not.
Student: We'd like to do that.
Landlord: Another thing is, in this district every household is arranged with a cleaner responsible for cleaning up the rubbish and recycling, but this service will cost you 15 dollars every month.
Student: What other bills do we have to pay?
Landlord: Bills for electricity, water and gas will also be covered by yourselves.
Student: OK.
Student: One more question, is air-conditioning available in the house?
Landlord: If you mean central air-conditioning, I'm afraid there isn't at the moment, but each room has a conditioning unit which is installed on the window.
Student: That will be fine.
Student: Anyway, I think it's a good place to live in.
Student: Can we go and see the house, please?
Landlord: Yes, of course.
Student: May I have your name and phone number?
Landlord: Yes, I'm Sam Dressler.
Landlord: That's S-A-M, D-R-E-double S-L-E-R.
Student: OK, Sam Dressler, I've got that down.
Landlord: And my phone number is 6753 4571.
Student: Thank you very much.
Student: I'll contact you after discussing it with my two friends.
Landlord: No problem.
Landlord: Bye!
Student: Bye!
